3.happening: We sort nums from smallest to largest.
3.why: Sorting lets us move pointers by comparing sums and skip duplicates easily.
3.invariant: After this line nums is in non-decreasing order.

4.happening: We create result as an empty list.
4.why: Each zero-sum triplet we discover will be appended here.
4.invariant: result holds only distinct triplets that sum to zero.

5.happening: The anchor index i advances to the next value.
5.why: Every triplet is found relative to a fixed first value.
5.invariant: All triplets whose smallest index is below i have already been recorded.

6.happening: We check whether this anchor equals the previous value.
6.why: Reusing an equal anchor would only reproduce triplets we already found.
6.invariant: Each distinct anchor value is processed at most once.

7.happening: The anchor repeats the previous value, so we skip it.
7.why: Skipping duplicate anchors keeps the result free of repeated triplets.
7.invariant: No duplicate triplet is ever appended.

8.happening: The left pointer starts just after the anchor.
8.why: The other two numbers must come from the range to the right of the anchor.
8.invariant: left is always greater than i.

9.happening: The right pointer starts at the last index.
9.why: Beginning at the extremes lets the sum be tuned by moving inward.
9.invariant: right is always the largest available index for this anchor.

10.happening: We continue while left and right have not crossed.
10.why: Once the pointers meet, no more pairs remain for this anchor.
10.invariant: left < right for every pair examined.

11.happening: We add the anchor and both pointer values into total.
11.why: The sign of this sum tells us which pointer to move.
11.invariant: total equals nums[i] + nums[left] + nums[right] for the current pointers.

12.happening: We test whether total is below zero.
12.why: A sum that is too small needs a larger contribution.
12.invariant: If total is negative, only a larger left value can help.

13.happening: The sum was too small, so left steps right to a larger value.
13.why: In a sorted array, moving left rightward increases the sum.
13.invariant: Moving left never decreases the running sum.

14.happening: We test whether total is above zero.
14.why: A sum that is too large needs a smaller contribution.
14.invariant: If total is positive, only a smaller right value can help.

15.happening: The sum was too large, so right steps left to a smaller value.
15.why: In a sorted array, moving right leftward decreases the sum.
15.invariant: Moving right never increases the running sum.

17.happening: The sum is exactly zero, so we build the triplet.
17.why: These three values are a valid answer to record.
17.invariant: The three highlighted cells sum to zero.

18.happening: We append the triplet to result.
18.why: This is one of the distinct triplets the problem asks for.
18.invariant: result now contains this zero-sum triplet.

19.happening: The left pointer advances past the value just used.
19.why: The current pair is finished, so we look for the next one.
19.invariant: left keeps increasing within this anchor's sweep.

20.happening: The right pointer retreats past the value just used.
20.why: Both ends of the found pair must change to seek a new pair.
20.invariant: right keeps decreasing within this anchor's sweep.

21.happening: We check whether the new left value repeats the one just used.
21.why: An identical left value would create a duplicate triplet.
21.invariant: left stops only on a value different from the one just recorded.

22.happening: The left value repeated, so we skip it.
22.why: Skipping equal values keeps the answer free of duplicate triplets.
22.invariant: Consecutive equal left values are collapsed into one.

23.why: Every anchor has been swept, so all triplets are found.
23.invariant: result is the complete set of distinct zero-sum triplets.

init.happening: Sort the array and prepare the result list.
init.why: Sorting is what makes the two-pointer sweep and duplicate-skipping possible.
init.invariant: nums is sorted and result is empty.

loop.happening: Advance an anchor or continue the inner sweep.
loop.why: The outer loop fixes a first value; the inner loop pairs the rest.
loop.invariant: The pointers describe one concrete candidate triplet at a time.

check.happening: Test a duplicate anchor or the sign of the running sum.
check.why: These tests decide whether to skip, move a pointer, or record a triplet.
check.invariant: Only genuinely new, zero-sum triplets are kept.

update.happening: Compute the sum or record a triplet.
update.why: Each step either measures the current pair or stores a valid answer.
update.invariant: The recorded triplets always sum to zero.

move.happening: Slide a pointer inward or over a duplicate.
move.why: Pointer moves tune the sum toward zero and skip repeats.
move.invariant: left only rises and right only falls within one anchor's sweep.

return.invariant: result contains every distinct zero-sum triplet.
